page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs, corresponding to 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64 and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 11 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across multiple domains. The instance achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2145.0 and a multi-core score of 19542.0. Memory bandwidth is notable, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 3834.86 GB/sec and large block uncached speeds at 191.08 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, it demonstrates high throughput with 87.32 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 80.84 GB/sec in static web serving. For LLM inference using a 135M model, it processes prompts at 2994.41 tokens per second and generates text at 340.25 tokens per second.

The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage attachments for data-heavy workloads and limiting its utility for hardware-accelerated deep learning. However, the inclusion of advanced CPU instruction sets, such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, helps mitigate the lack of a GPU for smaller AI workloads, as evidenced by strong performance in medium-sized LLM inference. This hardware profile is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, database operations, software compilation, and text processing.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It delivers 48 vCPUs clocked at 3.9 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz. While it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it features advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks show strong capabilities, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 19542.0, a Redis SET throughput of 87.32 million operations per second, and 80.84 GB/sec in static web serving. It also performs well in LLM inference, processing 7B model prompts at 260.79 tokens per second. This instance is optimized for general-purpose applications, database operations, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 11 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database operations and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.12xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 192 GB DDR5 RAM for balanced compute and memory workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
192.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
11 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Strong performance in database operations, web serving, and software compilation
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
